allow: Bash(PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python skills/codex-ws-client/scripts/codex_ws_client.py --json "There''s a growing trend of using CLI tools as an alternative to MCP \(Model Context Protocol\) for AI agent tool integration. Instead of building MCP servers, teams are wrapping existing CLI tools and letting AI agents invoke them via shell commands. Is this a genuine architectural shift, or just a shortcut that will cause problems at scale? Give me your honest, direct take.")

Bash(PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python skills/codex-ws-client/scripts/codex_ws_client.py --json --thread-id "019cdf02-65b4-7fb0-ad58-c8d5f1043624" "Summarize what you said in your previous response about CLI vs MCP.")

Bash(PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python skills/codex-ws-client/scripts/codex_ws_client.py --json --thread-id "019cdf02-65b4-7fb0-ad58-c8d5f1043624" "I agree with your warning that adding contracts, registries, and versioning to CLIs risks rebuilding MCP from scratch — that''s a real trap.:*)

Bash(PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python skills/codex-ws-client/scripts/codex_ws_client.py --json --thread-id "019cdf02-65b4-7fb0-ad58-c8d5f1043624" "I agree CLIs risk rebuilding MCP if you add contracts and registries. But I disagree that CLIs are fundamentally inferior. MCP is less than 2 years old and every MCP server is a network service to run, secure, and monitor. The real question is where the schema lives. Tools like Click and Cobra already emit structured metadata - if AI tooling standardized on parsing those, CLIs become self-describing without MCP infrastructure. Is MCP complexity only justified above a certain scale threshold?")
